summary: Tested bulk acquisition of MetObjects.csv from the runner via 4 routes (ranged raw fetch, LFS batch API, direct byte fetch, historical plain-blob fetch through promisor clone) — all blocked; the repo's entire history is LFS-migrated pointers and the LFS storage host is outside the egress allowlist. Built experiments/met_tail.py (stdlib query CLI: tail/share/tags/show) and experiments/met_fixture.jsonl (1 real CC0 record + 11 marked-synthetic records); all subcommands tested.
third_party_note: One CC0 JSON record (Met objectID 45734) from metmuseum.github.io docs into the fixture; recorded in THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md.
environment_note: Allowlist is github.com exactly; *.githubusercontent.com blocked (raw + LFS storage). LFS batch API reachable but useless. Assistant fetch provenance-gated; code-block URLs do not enter provenance. Reusable probe technique recorded in RESEARCH_LOG (blob:none promisor clone + cat-file -s).
next: Run 6: promisor-clone probe of github.com/Smithsonian/OpenAccess for plain-blob CC0 metadata; if plain, fetch one chunk and convert to met_tail JSONL (real data); if LFS/blocked, pivot condition fully triggered — acquisition-first rescreen of shelved directions.
